items.title: SEED: Self-Evolving On-Policy Distillation for Agentic Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: Large language models are increasingly trained as interactive agents for long-horizon tasks involving multi-turn interaction, tool use, and environment feedback. Outcome-based reinforcement learning (RL) provides a practical optimization paradigm, but its sparse trajectory-level

items.title: Video = World + Event Stream
items.summary: We present Wan-Streamer v0.3, which reframes our native-streaming interaction model under a single organizing view: a video is a world plus an event stream. The world is the persistent context in which a video unfolds, including the environment, scene, subjects, ambient acoustic

items.title: VideoChat3: Fully Open Video MLLM for Efficient and Generalist Video Understanding
items.summary: Recent advances in video understanding have spanned motion, long video, and streaming interaction, driving this field toward real-world applications. Despite this progress, current open-source models remain limited in several ways. They often struggle to generalize across diverse

items.title: RoboTTT: Context Scaling for Robot Policies
items.summary: Recent robot foundation models operate with single-step or short-history visuomotor context. We introduce Test-Time-Training Robot Policies (RoboTTT), a robot model and training recipe that scale visuomotor context to 8K timesteps, three orders of magnitude beyond state-of-the-ar

items.title: T^2MLR: Transformer with Temporal Middle-Layer Recurrence
items.summary: Transformer reasoning is limited by autoregressive decoding, which repeat edly compresses rich hidden computation through token space and makes it difficult for intermediate reasoning states to persist across time. We in troduce Transformers with Temporal Middle-Layer Recurrence

items.title: xHC: Expanded Hyper-Connections
items.summary: Hyper-Connections (HC) expand the residual stream of Transformers into $N$ parallel streams, providing a form of memory scaling beyond model width and depth. Manifold-Constrained HC (mHC) stabilizes this formulation at scale. The large gains from $N{=}1$ to $N{=}4$ suggest residu

items.title: DriftWorld: Fast World Modeling through Drifting
items.summary: Predictive world models enable robots to plan by imagining the outcomes of their actions, but their value for control hinges on generating many rollouts quickly. This creates a bottleneck for diffusion-based world models: multistep sampling makes each rollout expensive, limiting

items.title: On-Policy Delta Distillation
items.summary: On-policy distillation is an alternative post-training method in reinforcement learning that alleviates the constraints imposed by reward models by providing token-level supervision from a teacher model. Although on-policy distillation has been studied and applied across various

items.title: Scaling Behavior Foundation Model for Humanoid Robots
items.summary: Humanoid control requires natural whole-body coordination, precise real-time responses to control signals, and robust generalization across diverse environmental contexts, making it a cornerstone for generalist embodied agents. Behavior Foundation Models (BFMs) have recently emer

items.summary: The cover ratio measures how many dollars of investor orders a bond deal receives for every dollar of bonds issued.

items.summary: To gauge how much AI affects a job, researchers rate it from 0 to 1 based on how many of its tasks could be, or already are, done with AI.
